source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The discovery of the antiproton \( \overline{\text{p}} \) (a particle with the same rest energy as a proton, \( 938 \text{ MeV} \), but with the opposite electric charge) took place in 1956 through the following reaction:\[ \text{p} + \text{p} \rightarrow \text{p} + \text{p} + \text{p} + \overline{\text{p}} \]in which accelerated protons were incident on a target of protons at rest in the laboratory. The minimum incident kinetic energy needed to produce the reaction is called the \textit{threshold} kinetic energy, for which the final particles move together as if they were a single unit as shown in figure. Find the threshold kinetic energy to produce antiprotons in this reaction.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate notable visual reliance
question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows one valid image asset exists
multi_step: solving threshold kinetic energy for p + p -> p + p + p + pbar typically requires combining multiple conditions: rest energies, threshold condition, and relativistic conservation
question asks for a derived numeric threshold rather than direct lookup
multiple-choice options are close in scale, suggesting nontrivial calculation rather than single-step extraction
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric value and unit: 5.628 GeV
choices are provided and the answer matches one option exactly
physics result is checkable by independent calculation from the stated reaction and particle rest energy
registry_passed=true with question and answer both present

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和有效图片均存在，registry_passed=true，且 issues 为空
综合质量较高：multi_step_score=0.84，verifiability_score=0.94，适合作为可验证的多步物理题候选
图像依赖有支撑但不存在缺图风险：题目提到图像且 image_manifest 中有可用主图
风险标记仅为轻度注意项（图像必要性边界、单位归一化），不足以降低到 low_priority

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
The discovery of the antiproton \( \overline{\text{p}} \) (a particle with the same rest energy as a proton, \( 938 \text{ MeV} \), but with the opposite electric charge) took place in 1956 through the following reaction:
\[
\text{p} + \text{p} \rightarrow \text{p} + \text{p} + \text{p} + \overline{\text{p}}
\]
in which accelerated protons were incident on a target of protons at rest in the laboratory. The minimum incident kinetic energy needed to produce the reaction is called the \textit{threshold} kinetic energy, for which the final particles move together as if they were a single unit, as shown in the figure. Find the threshold kinetic energy to produce antiprotons in this reaction.

Preserved the explicit reference to the figure without adding unstated visual details.
